highlights.title: Beyond Score Prediction: LLM-Based Essay Scoring and Feedback Generation via Reinforcement Learning with Rubric Rewards
highlights.summary: RLAES optimizes essay scoring and feedback generation
highlights.detail: RLAES uses reinforcement learning and Rubric-based Feedback Evaluation for measurable feedback quality. Adaptive Gated Feedback Optimization reduces evaluation overhead.

highlights.title: Agents in the Wild: Where Research Meets Deployment
highlights.summary: Agentic systems transition from research to deployment
highlights.detail: The tutorial explores advances in reasoning, planning, and multi-agent coordination, highlighting open challenges and design patterns for successful deployment.

highlights.title: Two-Level Meta-Rubrics for Evaluating Open-Ended Generation: GAMUT, a Benchmark for Factual Completeness
highlights.detail: GAMUT measures factual completeness in long-form generation, addressing the limitations of existing precision-focused approaches.

highlights.title: Formal Verification of an Out-of-Order Multiprocessor against an In-Order Weak-Memory ISA
highlights.detail: The formal verification process addresses the challenges of inter-core interleaving and intra-core out-of-order execution. This research contributes to the development of more reliable and efficient processor designs.

highlights.title: Claude Is Not a Compiler
highlights.detail: The distinction between Claude and a compiler highlights the importance of understanding the roles and limitations of different technologies. This clarification can help prevent misconceptions and ensure appropriate application of tools.

items.title: Beyond Score Prediction: LLM-Based Essay Scoring and Feedback Generation via Reinforcement Learning with Rubric Rewards
items.summary: RLAES optimizes essay scoring and feedback generation
items.detail: RLAES uses reinforcement learning and Rubric-based Feedback Evaluation for measurable feedback quality. Adaptive Gated Feedback Optimization reduces evaluation overhead.

items.title: Agents in the Wild: Where Research Meets Deployment
items.summary: Agentic systems transition from research to deployment
items.detail: The tutorial explores advances in reasoning, planning, and multi-agent coordination, highlighting open challenges and design patterns for successful deployment.

items.title: Two-Level Meta-Rubrics for Evaluating Open-Ended Generation: GAMUT, a Benchmark for Factual Completeness
items.summary: GAMUT evaluates open-ended generation factuality
items.detail: GAMUT measures factual completeness in long-form generation, addressing the limitations of existing precision-focused approaches.

items.title: David Vélez and Robin Vince join the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC
items.summary: David Vélez and Robin Vince join OpenAI boards
items.detail: The new board members bring global leadership experience in finance, technology, and governance.

items.title: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
items.summary: Gemini 3.6 Flash and other models introduced
items.detail: The new Gemini models offer improved performance and capabilities.

items.title: Introducing the ChatGPT for small business program
items.summary: ChatGPT for Small Businesses program launched
items.detail: The program helps entrepreneurs build AI skills and automate work with ChatGPT.

items.title: OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation
items.summary: OpenAI and Hugging Face address security incident
items.detail: The incident highlights advanced cyber capabilities and lessons for defenders.

items.title: Grabette: an open system to record robot-manipulation data
items.summary: Grabette records robot-manipulation data
items.detail: Grabette is designed for robot-manipulation data collection and analysis.

items.title: The State of Simulation for Physical AI: An Overview
items.summary: The State of Simulation for Physical AI overviewed
items.detail: The overview discusses the current state and future directions of simulation for Physical AI.

items.title: Model: GnLOLot/MiniCPM5-1B-Claude-Opus-Fable5-V2-Thinking-GGUF
items.summary: GnLOLot/MiniCPM5-1B-Claude model released
items.detail: The model is a text-generation model with a large number of downloads and likes.

items.title: [$] Debating the role of large language models in the kernel community
items.summary: Kernel community debates LLM role
items.detail: The discussion involves LLM attribution, code-review tools, and ethics concerns.

items.title: A Smarter DIY Air Filter
items.summary: AirSense is a DIY air filter
items.detail: AirSense is a clever solution for maintaining a healthy indoor habitat.

items.title: Security updates for Tuesday
items.summary: Security updates issued by AlmaLinux, Debian, Fedora, Mageia, Oracle
items.detail: Updates include fixes for various packages such as httpd, libtiff, and python3.14. These updates are crucial for maintaining system security and preventing potential vulnerabilities.

items.title: Firefox 153 released
items.summary: Firefox 153.0 released with new features
items.detail: This release includes changes to default local-file-access permissions for extensions and a visual indicator for location access. These updates aim to enhance user security and experience.

items.title: Counterfeit Retro Mainboards with Fake AGP Slots Are a Thing
items.summary: Counterfeit retro mainboards with fake AGP slots found
items.detail: The discovery of counterfeit retro mainboards highlights the importance of verifying component authenticity, especially in niche markets. This can help prevent potential compatibility issues and financial losses.

items.title: Neural Net Reads the Gas Meter
items.summary: Neural net reads gas meter
items.detail: The application of neural networks in reading gas meters demonstrates the potential of AI in automating tasks and improving efficiency. This technology can be applied to various industries for enhanced accuracy and convenience.

items.title: Formal Verification of an Out-of-Order Multiprocessor against an In-Order Weak-Memory ISA
items.summary: Formal verification of out-of-order multiprocessor
items.detail: The formal verification process addresses the challenges of inter-core interleaving and intra-core out-of-order execution. This research contributes to the development of more reliable and efficient processor designs.

items.title: Claude Is Not a Compiler
items.summary: Claude is not a compiler
items.detail: The distinction between Claude and a compiler highlights the importance of understanding the roles and limitations of different technologies. This clarification can help prevent misconceptions and ensure appropriate application of tools.

items.title: Build-Authorized Evidence for Opaque Calls: A Fail-Closed Rewrite-Authority Boundary
items.summary: Build-authorized evidence for opaque calls
items.detail: The introduction of build-authorized path-effect interfaces aims to enforce rewrite authority boundaries. This approach enhances the security and reliability of compiler rewrites by ensuring that only authorized facts are considered.

items.title: A User-oriented Portable, Reproducible, and Scalable Software Ecosystem
items.summary: Portable, reproducible, and scalable software ecosystem
items.detail: The development of a unified command-line interface facilitates interaction with user-workflows across various hardware platforms. This ecosystem promotes flexibility, reproducibility, and scalability in computational workflows.

items.title: Enabling Multi-Dimensional Distributed Trace Comparison with Contrast
items.summary: Multi-dimensional distributed trace comparison with Contrast
items.detail: The introduction of Contrast enables the comparison of distributed traces across multiple dimensions. This system aids in the diagnosis of system behavior by capturing structural, temporal, and semantic properties of trace populations.

items.title: Searching for Plans You Can Actually Build: A Realizability-Aware Full-Space Optimizer for MoE Training and Serving
items.summary: Realizability-aware full-space optimizer for MoE training and serving
items.detail: The moefs optimizer addresses the challenge of deployment realizability in MoE systems. By making deployment realizability a first-class search constraint, moefs enhances the efficiency and practicality of MoE training and serving.

items.title: NVIDIA/cosmos-framework
items.summary: NVIDIA/cosmos-framework for inference and training
items.detail: The NVIDIA/cosmos-framework provides a foundation for running Cosmos Models. This framework is designed to support both inference and training, facilitating the development and deployment of AI models.

items.title: google/benchmark
items.summary: Google/benchmark for microbenchmark support
items.detail: The google/benchmark library offers a set of tools for creating and running microbenchmarks. This library is designed to help developers optimize and evaluate the performance of their code.

items.title: huxingyi/autoremesher
items.summary: Automatic quad remeshing tool available
items.detail: This tool can be used for various 3D modeling tasks, providing an efficient way to remesh quad models.

items.title: ml-explore/mlx
items.summary: MLX framework for Apple silicon released
items.detail: MLX is designed to work with Apple silicon, providing an array framework for machine learning tasks.

items.title: rtk-ai/rtk
items.summary: RTK CLI proxy reduces LLM token consumption
items.detail: This proxy is a single Rust binary with zero dependencies, making it a lightweight solution for reducing token consumption.

items.title: Alishahryar1/free-claude-code
items.summary: Free Claude code access from terminal or IDE
items.detail: This project allows users to access Claude code, Codex, or Pi for free, similar to OpenClaw, with voice support.
